164172.explanation: All residents can voice their opinions via oudeijsselstreekspreekt.nl and surveys are held on important topics. This is much more accessible than a referendum.

164173.explanation: Residents who want to manage a small park can create more involvement and meet each other together. An appropriate allowance can help cover costs, but it mainly remains a way to strengthen local ownership and care for the living environment.

164174.explanation: Lower property tax is good for residents and entrepreneurs. We want taxes down where possible but not at the cost of important facilities. The municipality must therefore carefully weigh how much room there is.

164176.explanation: Oude IJsselstreek is rich in advisory interest groups and resident groups who have been contributing for years. All residents can also give input via oudeijsselstreekspreekt.nl. It is important to respect their role and strengthen it first before setting up new structures.

164177.explanation: A higher tourist tax seems little meaningful. It makes overnight stays more expensive without clear added value. Lokaal Belang is not in favor of additionally charging the visitors of our beautiful area.

164178.explanation: The municipality must spend less than it receives to build reserves and pay off debts. That is prudent financial management. At the same time, we must continue investing in what is needed for residents and villages.

164179.explanation: Our companies want to expand and need more space. Additional business parks are needed to enable that growth because it is good for our local economy and employment. Lokaal Belang believes the municipality must ensure careful integration into the landscape.

164182.explanation: Local energy initiatives deserve support. Various subsidies already exist that encourage cooperation and sustainable generation. Lokaal Belang finds it important to help residents carry out their plans. Subsidies for this can be requested via the Energieloket Achterhoek.

164183.explanation: Lokaal Belang supports the role of the DRU but remains critical about financial management. Entrepreneurship and efficient cooperation are necessary for a healthy future. The DRU has been given a final chance. They must not receive extra money from the municipality.

164185.explanation: Lokaal Belang supports free bulky waste disposal, but only if it can be done without sharply increasing waste collection charges.

164186.explanation: Less mowing strengthens biodiversity and saves costs. Playing fields and recreational facilities must still be properly mowed. The municipality must weigh per location where this is possible without compromising traffic safety, accessibility, or the streetscape.

164187.explanation: For two years there's been a national 'no, unless' policy for solar parks on agricultural land: roofs and unused sites are prioritized to protect scarce land. The municipality must adhere to these rules but respect ongoing applications.

164188.explanation: The health of residents is always paramount. Strict rules apply to the use of pesticides. It is unnecessary to establish extra regulations on this.

164190.explanation: Lokaal Belang supports research into cheaper waste processing. If arranged so that residents no longer have to separate waste themselves, this creates a win-win situation.

164191.explanation: An off-leash dog area can be valuable if residents and volunteers want to take the initiative and manage it themselves. The municipality does not need to establish them everywhere but should be accommodating and cooperate when there is local support and initiative.

164192.explanation: The health of residents is always paramount. Strict rules apply to the use of pesticides, and farmers are under great pressure. Farmers manage our countryside, and the municipality should not impose additional restrictions beyond strict national regulations.

164193.explanation: According to regional agreements, Oude IJsselstreek meets its target for wind energy. As far as Local Interest is concerned, that is sufficient.

164194.explanation: The municipality should not spend more money on integration than necessary. The national government provides a specific budget for this, and the municipality must adhere to that without allocating extra funds.

164195.explanation: Shelter is only negotiable for Local Interest if strict conditions are met regarding safety, care, and nuisance. Without these guarantees, we will not agree.

164196.explanation: The municipality must continue investing in school buildings in the coming years. Some schools are outdated or not future-proof. By improving them now in a targeted and sustainable way, we ensure our children can develop in a safe and modern learning environment.

164197.explanation: Attention to the dialect is important for culture and identity. Schools are responsible for the curriculum, but the municipality must support initiatives so that Achterhooks also gets a place in education. In care, it also helps to build a bridge between young and old people.

164198.explanation: Independent, reliable, and accessible local journalism is indispensable for the municipality. That is why the municipality must continue supporting the local broadcaster.

164199.explanation: Local Interest supports the role of the DRU but remains critical of its financial management. Entrepreneurship and efficient cooperation are necessary for a healthy future. The DRU has been given one last chance. If it fails now, bankruptcy is inevitable.

164200.explanation: Local Interest sees primary schools not only as places to learn but also as important meeting places in the neighborhood or village. Local Interest encourages the development of other functions within school buildings, such as childcare, sports, culture, care, libraries, or a neighborhood service point.

164201.explanation: Local Interest finds it important that children can swim well for safety and health. The municipality can support this when it aligns well with the schools’ plans.

164202.explanation: The municipality must commit to future-proof sports parks and merged clubs. A new sports park for S.V. Wisch fits within this, but other merged clubs also have these wishes. The municipality must carefully weigh where the funding goes.

164208.explanation: Sufficient social rental housing must be built, but there is especially a need for good, affordable owner-occupied homes. More of all types of housing should be built, but the ratio is acceptable for Local Interest.

164210.explanation: Local Interest supports a self-occupancy obligation. Those who buy a home must live in it themselves. This keeps homes available for local residents. Exceptions are possible when a home is deliberately built for rental, such as by housing corporations.

164211.explanation: Converting vacant buildings makes a valuable contribution to solving the housing shortage, according to Local Interest. The municipality should certainly encourage this, but the investment should lie with the developers and owners themselves.

164212.explanation: Allowing permanent residence can counter vacancy and add housing, but only under conditions. For example, the home and surrounding infrastructure must be suitable.

164214.explanation: More housing is needed for both elderly and young people. Both apartments and life-course-proof homes with gardens contribute to solving the housing problem.

164215.explanation: Permitting a second home in the garden helps address the housing shortage. However, consideration must be given to garden space and preventing nuisance to neighbors.

164216.explanation: By purchasing land around village centers itself, the municipality can speed up housing construction. If we want to achieve the agreed 2,500 homes quickly, active land policy is needed to accelerate and preserve space for businesses and facilities.

164217.explanation: The municipality now opts for customized and targeted support, ensuring help goes to those who really need it. Raising the income threshold sounds sympathetic but does not always help those who need support the most.

164218.explanation: Mandatory social work for welfare recipients is undesirable. It can be stigmatizing and ignores personal circumstances. The focus should be on targeted guidance to suitable work or societal participation.

164220.explanation: Informal caregivers form an indispensable link in our society. They provide long-term, unpaid care to loved ones with illness, disability, or need. Informal care is always tailored, and for Local Interest, exemption from the job application requirement should be proportionate to the care burden.

164222.explanation: Having low-income residents reapply every year creates unnecessary hassle and helps no one. It costs time, causes stress, and makes the system unnecessarily heavy. A simpler approach provides people with more peace and certainty.

164223.explanation: Extra support for lower-income people makes it easier to insulate their homes faster and more comfortably. Schemes already exist that ensure households with limited budgets can also benefit from lower energy bills and more sustainable homes.

164224.explanation: More charging stations should only be placed when residents themselves have a need for them. At the same time, there must remain sufficient parking spaces for cars without plugs, so everyone has enough parking available.

164225.explanation: Good public transport to other areas in the Netherlands and Germany is indispensable for the region. The fastest route has no national borders. Residents, especially students, must be able to reach locations quickly, safely, and affordably.

164226.explanation: A general 30 km/h zone goes too far. It is wiser to reduce speed only where truly necessary for safety and livability, so measures remain targeted and traffic flows well.

164227.explanation: Barriers in rural areas hinder agricultural traffic and often solve little. Only at genuinely unsafe points can it be an option, but we prefer other safety measures. Accessibility for emergency services and agricultural traffic is paramount.

164228.explanation: A total ban is undesirable. Destination traffic must always be able to reach villages. However, Local Interest supports prohibiting heavy through truck traffic where possible.

164231.explanation: Helping entrepreneurs make their buildings more accessible fits with our ambition to remain the most accessible municipality in the Netherlands. With targeted support, more businesses can take steps that make a difference for everyone. This must be standard in new construction.

164233.explanation: Local Interest stands for good youth care for those who need it. This sometimes requires extra effort by the municipality, but always with attention to financial responsibility and a clear call to the national government for structural contributions.

164172.explanation: We strongly support giving residents more say over their own living environment. They know best what is happening in their own area. We want to talk with residents rather than about residents.

164173.explanation: If residents want to take over management, that can be arranged, but only on a voluntary basis with a volunteer agreement with the municipality.

164174.explanation: We strive for the lowest possible property tax for residents and entrepreneurs. At the same time, we recognize that the municipality needs sufficient resources to invest in facilities such as safety, public space maintenance, care, and sustainability.

164176.explanation: We are in favor of a referendum but not of a citizens' assembly. In our view, there are too many complications with the latter.

164179.explanation: We want entrepreneurs to have enough space but do not want large-scale industrial parks with mega halls.

164182.explanation: There are national subsidies for this.

164183.explanation: We are not against the DRU itself, but we oppose that more money keeps going there without looking at the actual problems. It is taxpayer money being decided upon.

164186.explanation: Mowing promotes traffic safety. Poisonous ragwort for animals and dangerous grass awns for dogs should be combated as effectively as possible.

164188.explanation: Not a single farmer sprays poison on their land! The government has established strict rules for using crop protection products.

164191.explanation: We are strong supporters of an off-leash dog area. Let's start with one as a trial, maintained by volunteers in consultation with the municipality.

164192.explanation: Not a single farmer sprays poison on their land! The government has established strict rules for using crop protection products.

164193.explanation: Regarding large wind turbines, we are already ahead of other municipalities in our region. We are in favor of small wind turbines, for example at farms.

164195.explanation: We do not want to fulfill the task assigned to other municipalities on their behalf. As long as the Distribution Act applies, we want to meet the legal task by distributing people across the centers so that they can integrate well and to spread any nuisance.

164198.explanation: We believe it is important that a local broadcaster continues to exist. They provide news and information that national broadcasters do not cover, but which are important for the region. It is an indispensable link. Keep the standard amount and possibly provide custom solutions as needed.

164199.explanation: By letting the foundation go bankrupt, you do not put an end to the entire DRU. After all, the building remains standing and the staff will continue to be paid by the Employee Insurance Agency (UWV), allowing the DRU to continue. We consider that important.

164200.explanation: We are in favor of shared use, provided that is organizationally feasible. This can promote livability in the centers.

164201.explanation: The costs of swimming lessons can heavily burden the family budget. Residents should be able to apply for a contribution towards the costs.

164202.explanation: We are not opposed to this, but believe the overall picture must first be carefully considered.

164208.explanation: The target is 30%. Anything above that is a bonus, but let's first strive to reach 30%.

164210.explanation: In some situations we agree with this, and in others we do not.

164211.explanation: We welcome the conversion of vacant offices into homes, but the municipality must make the rules for this easier. However, without subsidies.

164215.explanation: There are too many ifs and buts here to give a well-founded answer.

164216.explanation: We do believe that the housing needs of each center should be considered and that it should first be examined whether building within the centers themselves is still possible.

164217.explanation: The municipality provides support to this group and we are in favor of customized solutions in that regard.

164218.explanation: We believe people should be encouraged to seek work, but also realize that this is not always possible. Again, customized solutions apply here.

164220.explanation: Informal caregiving should not be underestimated. It is necessary to look at the amount of informal care provided in order to determine whether there is room for paid employment.

164222.explanation: With the application, it must be carefully assessed whether the funds are structural. If they are structural, then it is not necessary.

164223.explanation: There are already various arrangements for this.

164224.explanation: However, the placement and need for charging stations must be carefully considered.

164226.explanation: To guarantee safety, we want more 30 km/h zones within built-up areas. However, this does not mean that all roads must have a 30 km/h limit.

164228.explanation: We want to keep through truck traffic out of the centers as much as possible, but a total ban is not achievable.

164231.explanation: However, we do support entrepreneurs receiving tips from the municipality when they want to adapt their building.

164233.explanation: Youth care should primarily be financed by the national government. However, we favor customized solutions for this group.

164172.explanation: There are sufficient opportunities to participate in the participation process. For example, residents can speak at meetings and/or attend information evenings.

164173.explanation: Maintenance by residents is not a sustainable solution. Public space is and remains a municipal responsibility.

164174.explanation: It is not realistic. Less property tax (OZB) also means less money for other things, such as subsidies.

164176.explanation: There are various advisory boards, among others in the Social Domain and Energy. The citizens' council could then have an advisory role alongside the usual participation and consultation.

164177.explanation: Indexation due to inflation is possible, but we must keep it attractive for tourists.

164178.explanation: Building up a reserve is wise to cover future setbacks or additional expenses.

164179.explanation: It is a pillar for our prosperity, employment, and wellbeing. We do not want to become a commuter town.

164182.explanation: One can make use of the existing subsidy policy in this area.

164185.explanation: Much waste can be handed in at Stichting Aktief. Green waste periodically at the municipal yard. Construction and demolition waste from renovations must be borne by the owner.

164186.explanation: This is already existing policy. Mowing even less is not desirable, among other reasons due to traffic safety.

164188.explanation: The market and consumers must be willing to pay a slightly higher price for organic products. If the municipality subsidizes, we get an uneven playing field.

164190.explanation: Conducting such an investigation provides insight into the costs.

164191.explanation: At this moment, the municipality is in talks with the initiator to find a suitable and affordable solution.

164192.explanation: We adhere to the national policy guidelines here.

164193.explanation: At this moment, Oude IJsselstreek has more than sufficiently met the RES 1.0 contribution requirement.

164194.explanation: Integration is an important aspect to be able to participate. Participation means counting!

164195.explanation: Provided that the established policy document is adhered to: strict conditions that the COA must comply with.

164196.explanation: The municipality is responsible for new construction. The maintenance of schools is up to the schools themselves.

164197.explanation: Dialect must be preserved. It is cultural heritage. Keep it going.

164198.explanation: The current contribution is sufficient at this moment.

164199.explanation: That is up to the foundation itself. We will continue to support the library, music school, pop venue, and other cultural events.

164200.explanation: It is the authority of school boards to allow this.

164201.explanation: Our principle is that every child learns to swim. At this moment, we cannot foresee the financial consequences.

164202.explanation: How much money and when will be determined in the future.

164208.explanation: We do not set a target percentage, but it will be more than the current 28%.

164211.explanation: Redeveloping real estate is not a municipal task. Generously cooperating with zoning plan changes: YES.

164212.explanation: The term says it all: a home during your vacation.

164215.explanation: The exception is placing a care home on one’s own property.

164216.explanation: We have included an active land policy in our program.

164217.explanation: The current poverty policy of our municipality is already generous. Further increases would create a significant gap in the budget.

164218.explanation: We operate on trust. Residents receiving benefits are also allowed to do volunteer work.

164220.explanation: Many working people are also informal caregivers. Being a caregiver is independent from receiving welfare benefits and the job application obligation.

164222.explanation: This depends on the situation. Whether it is about extra funds for a studying child or otherwise. Applying for extra funds should be assessed based on our fundamental principle of trust.

164223.explanation: The current subsidy policy is adequate.

164225.explanation: Let it be investigated whether this is profitable.

164226.explanation: The road design and location determine if a 30 km/h zone will be implemented. Traffic safety is paramount. This also applies to scooters, fatbikes, and speedbikes.

164227.explanation: The design of the road and the allowed speed are determined by the road’s layout.

164228.explanation: It is not always avoidable. For example, there is destination traffic.

164172.explanation: A referendum often pits groups against each other. It also does not lead to careful decision-making. In a municipality, a referendum cannot be binding. Therefore, a referendum often raises false expectations.

164173.explanation: Involving residents in green management strengthens social cohesion and increases the sense of ownership. We consider this form of cooperation important.

164174.explanation: The municipality must strive for the lowest possible property tax (OZB) without compromising facilities.

164176.explanation: A citizens’ council is important because it allows residents to easily and directly participate in policy discussions. It strengthens democracy and trust. It also helps to align policies and decisions better with what our residents really need.

164177.explanation: We want to keep the municipality attractive for tourism and use the income from this tax accordingly. The tourist tax was recently increased (for 2026). We follow the Achterhoek regional average to keep roughly in line with other municipalities in the area.

164178.explanation: No more taxes should be collected than necessary to cover expenditures. A surplus (excess reserves) without a clear purpose does not fit municipal finances, but the municipality must be frugal and prudent with money. The province supervises this.

164179.explanation: Through regional agreements, the municipality works on ensuring sufficient business parks. Developments do not stand still and new space will be needed in the future.

164182.explanation: Financial support lowers barriers to sustainability and makes our residents more self-reliant. Municipal subsidies for sustainable measures already exist and we want to make these available to cooperating residents as well.

164183.explanation: The council has agreed to a financial arrangement for the DRU Industrial Park foundation. We have agreed to this proposal and follow the line of the council proposal and the financial resources agreed therein.

164185.explanation: Residents can already have household items collected for free through Kringloop Aktief. Pruning waste can be deposited free of charge at the municipality. For residual bulky waste and construction and demolition waste, collection through local waste collectors is a suitable option.

164186.explanation: Less mowing promotes biodiversity, provides more living space for insects and birds, and contributes to a healthier living environment. However, sufficient attention must be paid to traffic safety.

164187.explanation: A complete ban goes too far. We advocate stricter enforcement of the solar ladder: first use roofs and unused plots. We want to allow opportunities for small-scale, well-integrated fields with local support and at least 50% ownership.

164188.explanation: We want to promote nature-inclusive and organic forms of agriculture, but do not see it as the municipality’s role to grant subsidies to agricultural entrepreneurs.

164190.explanation: If it appears that new technologies operate more cheaply and waste streams become sustainably reusable, we are open to that.

164191.explanation: Contributes to livability, wellbeing, and a clean public space. Provided there is a logical location and clear agreements.

164192.explanation: The use of crop protection products carries risks for people and the environment. We want to protect residents from these risks where possible.

164193.explanation: Without wind turbines it is not possible to achieve the goals set out in the Regional Energy Strategy. We advocate for the most suitable locations, strong local involvement, and as much local ownership as possible.

164194.explanation: By spending money on the integration of newcomers, they can participate more quickly in our society. This makes our community stronger and more social.

164195.explanation: We adhere to the agreements from the policy document adopted by the council.

164196.explanation: Children have the right to a safe, healthy, and modern learning environment. We are proud of IKC (Integrated Child Center) de Wijssel in Gendringen and strive for the same quality at IKC Silvolde and IKC de Woelwaters Ulft.

164197.explanation: We cherish the Achterhoek dialect, our culture, and regional identity. As a council, we do not decide on education at schools, but we support giving attention to the Achterhoek dialect.

164198.explanation: We stand for independent journalism in our region. Through a local broadcaster, we have regional coverage of news from the area.

164199.explanation: It is an indispensable cultural and social facility. We must strengthen it, not dismantle it!

164200.explanation: We welcome this, but it must always be done in cooperation with the schools. It must never come at the expense of the education and learning environment of the students.

164201.explanation: School swimming fits with what we stand for: a fair start, equal opportunities, and a society in which every child can participate. We also support financial contributions for families for whom swimming lessons are too large a financial burden.

164202.explanation: Sports bring residents together. Sports contribute to physical and mental health. We advocate for strong clubs and future-proof facilities. Where clubs work on their future, we invest alongside them.

164208.explanation: Everyone should be able to live affordably. In many housing plans, there are still too few affordable homes.

164210.explanation: We agree with this statement. Anyone who buys a home in our municipality must live there themselves. A house is primarily a place to live, not a toy for others to make a profit from.

164211.explanation: It is a shame to leave buildings empty while the housing shortage is growing. Therefore, the municipality must actively focus on transforming, among other things, vacant offices. This way, living space can be created relatively quickly.

164212.explanation: Using holiday homes as a permanent address does not fit within the zoning plans and existing infrastructure. It puts local livability under pressure. Local tailor-made solutions remain possible via the municipal council.

164214.explanation: Many elderly people are looking for lifetime-adapted homes. Currently, there are too few of these. Building homes for the elderly also creates flow-through opportunities for younger generations.

164215.explanation: This contributes to solving the housing shortage. Current policies already offer possibilities for this, such as temporary care housing. Good integration into the environment remains necessary.

164216.explanation: To be able to build quickly and affordably, the municipality may need to seize the opportunity to purchase suitable land.

164217.explanation: Poverty is becoming more prolonged and more people struggle to make ends meet after paying fixed expenses. We want to raise the income threshold (130% of the social assistance norm) so that more families can be supported.

164218.explanation: As long as the human dimension is leading here! Volunteer work, social activities in a neighborhood, or a paid job. We see opportunities but only if it suits and works for the resident.

164220.explanation: As of January 1, 2026, a new Participation Act states that residents who provide intensive informal care are exempt from the job application obligation. We agree with this.

164222.explanation: Residents with a low income who apply for additional resources no longer have to apply every year. This continues automatically. It should remain this way. We operate on trust and only conduct random checks.

164223.explanation: We help people who cannot pay their energy bills by extending and expanding current municipal subsidies.

164224.explanation: Residents are increasingly driving electrically and should be supported in this. This makes us less dependent on fossil fuels and is also better for air quality.

164225.explanation: Good public transport connections reduce car traffic, are sustainable, and strengthen the regional economy. From some of our villages, a route via Germany is a good and fast option.

164226.explanation: A lower speed increases traffic safety and livability.

164227.explanation: Traffic safety is important. Speed bumps are not our first preference but can sometimes be necessary to reduce speed and thereby prevent accidents.

164228.explanation: Heavy traffic, such as tractors and trucks, causes danger and nuisance. However, companies and shops must remain accessible. The municipality must therefore work on alternative, safe routes.

164231.explanation: Accessibility is part of good entrepreneurship. Entrepreneurs are responsible for making their shop or business accessible to everyone. This increases their customer base and contributes to a society where everyone belongs.

164233.explanation: Spending money is not an objective. We pursue quality care: every child or young person who needs help must receive good care and support.

164172.explanation: PRO! stands for thinking along, participating, and co-deciding. Referenda give residents the opportunity to influence important decisions between elections.

164173.explanation: PRO! stimulates residents’ involvement in their living environment. By letting residents take over the management of a park with appropriate compensation, participation and care for public spaces are strengthened.

164174.explanation: PRO! wants to keep the property tax (ozb) stable and affordable. Reduction or increase is only considered if necessary for a healthy municipal financial situation.

164176.explanation: PRO! stands for thinking along, participating, and co-deciding. A citizens’ assembly increases resident involvement and ensures better supported advice on important decisions.

164177.explanation: PRO! seeks a balance between municipal income and an attractive environment for visitors and entrepreneurs.

164178.explanation: PRO! wants a healthy financial administration with sufficient reserves for investments and setbacks.

164179.explanation: PRO! wants to offer space to entrepreneurs, but with attention to landscape, sustainability, and the living environment.

164182.explanation: PRO! supports local initiatives where residents jointly generate sustainable energy.

164183.explanation: PRO! does not want structural emergency aid without a solid financial foundation.

164185.explanation: PRO! wants to stimulate waste separation and reuse and prevent illegal dumping. At the same time, waste policy must remain affordable and feasible.

164186.explanation: PRO! wants to strengthen biodiversity and nature. By mowing verges and grass fields less frequently, more space is created for plants and insects, and green management becomes more sustainable.

164187.explanation: PRO! follows the solar ladder: first solar panels on roofs and in built-up areas. Solar panels on agricultural land are not excluded, but only if done carefully and on a small scale.

164188.explanation: PRO! wants to protect health and the living environment and stimulate sustainability. If farmers voluntarily refrain from using pesticides near buildings, financial compensation is appropriate.

164190.explanation: PRO! is open to research into more efficient and sustainable forms of waste processing. If post-separation can contribute to lower costs and better waste differentiation, it is wise to investigate this.

164191.explanation: PRO! finds it important that public spaces are pleasant and usable for everyone. Off-leash dog areas provide dogs with enough space and limit nuisance elsewhere in the villages.

164192.explanation: PRO! wants to protect health and the living environment. The municipality must strive to limit and, where possible, prohibit the use of pesticides near buildings.

164193.explanation: Sustainable energy is necessary. PRO! wants to cooperate with wind turbines at carefully chosen locations, with attention to landscape and participation.

164194.explanation: PRO! finds good integration important for social cohesion and participation. Cutting budgets here leads to bigger problems in the long term.

164195.explanation: PRO! wants to organize shelter in a humane and careful way, with distribution and support throughout society.

164196.explanation: Healthy and well-ventilated school buildings are important for the welfare and development of children.

164197.explanation: Attention to regional language strengthens cultural identity and connection with the region.

164198.explanation: A strong local broadcaster contributes to information provision, involvement, and democracy.

164199.explanation: A controlled bankruptcy with restart offers perspective for a healthy future.

164200.explanation: PRO! sees schools as important facilities in the neighborhood. By using primary schools in the evenings and weekends for social, cultural, or sports activities, livability is increased and existing buildings are used more efficiently.

164201.explanation: PRO! finds school swimming important for the safety and equal opportunities of children. By investing in this as a municipality, all children get the chance to learn to swim well.

164202.explanation: PRO! places great value on sport and exercise for everyone. Good sports facilities contribute to health, meeting others, and livability. Therefore, investing in a new sports park is justified.

164208.explanation: PRO! strives for at least one-third social rental housing in new construction to guarantee affordable housing for starters, young people, and people with lower incomes.

164210.explanation: PRO! wants to counter speculation and keep homes available for residents who want to live in them themselves.

164211.explanation: Reusing vacant offices helps reduce the housing shortage and prevents vacancy.

164212.explanation: PRO! wants homes to be used according to their original function and for the municipality to keep control over the housing supply.

164214.explanation: More suitable homes for the elderly promote flow and independent living.

164215.explanation: Small-scale second homes, such as caregiver housing, fit flexible and future-oriented housing policy.

164216.explanation: PRO! supports an active land policy so the municipality can control where, what, and for whom is built. By purchasing land around village centers, affordable homes, appropriate businesses, and balanced environmental development can be better ensured.

164217.explanation: PRO! wants to support more people who just exceed the current income limit. By raising this limit, silent poverty can be better addressed and more residents receive appropriate help.

164218.explanation: PRO! starts from trust and customization. Not everyone receiving social assistance can perform community work, and mandatory counter-performances do not help people move forward sustainably.

164220.explanation: PRO! values informal care highly. People who provide intensive care for a family member or acquaintance should not be burdened with a job application requirement but should be supported and appreciated.

164222.explanation: PRO! wants to relieve residents with low incomes. By granting arrangements automatically as much as possible, unnecessary bureaucracy is avoided and people receive the support they need faster.

164223.explanation: PRO! wants to counter energy poverty and make sustainability possible for everyone. Extra support for people with low incomes helps to insulate homes and reduce energy costs.

164224.explanation: More charging stations are needed to encourage sustainable transport. PRO! wants to make electric driving accessible for residents and visitors.

164225.explanation: A good public transport connection with Nijmegen and Arnhem improves accessibility, sustainability, and opportunities for work and education.

164226.explanation: Traffic safety for cyclists and pedestrians comes first. A maximum speed of 30 km/h increases safety and livability.

164227.explanation: Traffic safety is important, but measures must be assessed per situation and remain customized.

164228.explanation: Less heavy traffic in village centers increases traffic safety and reduces nuisance.

164231.explanation: PRO! finds it important that everyone can participate fully in society. Accessible buildings contribute to inclusion and equal opportunities. The municipality can support entrepreneurs to remove barriers for people with disabilities and make facilities reachable for everyone.

164233.explanation: PRO! believes children and young people should receive the care they need. If national funding falls short, the municipality must be willing to invest extra in youth care and prevention.

164172.explanation: Collaborating with our residents means being informed, thinking along, participating, or even co-deciding. We do this by regularly engaging with our residents and interest groups. In exceptional cases, a referendum is also an option.

164173.explanation: The VVD believes we must stimulate residents' initiatives. We primarily think of one-time contributions for starting initiatives and not structural budgets for maintenance.

164174.explanation: The VVD supports financial stability. Property taxes have risen in recent years. Given the current financial situation, we are satisfied if we can keep property taxes stable in the coming period. When there is room in our budget, we want to reduce property taxes to their previous level.

164176.explanation: The VVD finds it important that residents can express their opinions and that they are heard. We believe it is the municipal council's task to gather these opinions. Setting up a citizens' council is not cheap and, in our opinion, unnecessary.

164177.explanation: The VVD wants to encourage recreation in our municipality. With a higher tourist tax, we can generate more income and develop plans for the future.

164178.explanation: Sound financial policy is a core value of the VVD. Spending more than what comes in does not fit this principle.

164179.explanation: More business parks are important for employment in our municipality.

164182.explanation: We want to stimulate the generation of sustainable energy. But collective initiatives are not necessarily preferred over individual ones. We especially want to treat our residents equally and encourage them proportionally.

164183.explanation: We support a DRU that meets local needs. This means that we want to continue financially supporting local cultural and social activities. Larger ambitions should be financed by the DRU itself. We believe that the DRU should be able to manage with less money in the future.

164185.explanation: We believe that waste charges should be cost-covering. If disposing of bulky waste were made free, waste charges would have to rise significantly. This would result in all residents paying for it. We are not in favor of that.

164186.explanation: Promoting biodiversity is great, but safety is our top priority. Mowing less may lead to unsafe situations (and also nuisance) for the environment.

164187.explanation: We certainly want to encourage the energy transition. However, we think there are currently enough solar panels available. Moreover, we believe that all roofs should be fully utilized first.

164188.explanation: There is national policy on the use of pesticides. We do not want to complicate regulations locally with exceptions. Furthermore, the farmer is responsible for how he or she manages their land.

164190.explanation: The VVD is not in favor of the current waste separation system. Pre-sorting will never achieve 100% waste separation. Sorting afterwards is more favorable for residents and better for the environment. So that is always our preference.

164191.explanation: The VVD believes that pets can contribute significantly to well-being. Providing off-leash areas is not a primary task of the municipality. Of course, we are open to initiatives from society.

164192.explanation: The safety of our residents is paramount. National legislation oversees this. If we want to campaign for a ban, there must be a clear reason (regarding health), which we do not see at present.

164193.explanation: Sustainable energy is an important principle for the VVD. However, our municipality largely meets the current regional targets for wind energy.

164194.explanation: Integrating newcomers is a legal task. It is important that newcomers integrate into our society as quickly as possible to also contribute.

164195.explanation: The VVD believes we must comply with the Dispersion Act. In its implementation, we mainly consider the impact on our residents. Therefore, one location in the rural area is our preference. When choosing the final location, it is crucial for us that plans limit any nuisance.

164196.explanation: Education is important for our party. Housing for education is a municipal task. We believe that sufficient investments have been made in the quality of buildings in recent years.

164197.explanation: We are proud of our culture and dialect. We welcome attention to this language, but the content of the curriculum is the responsibility of the school itself.

164198.explanation: We emphasize the importance of a local broadcaster. Currently, financial support for this is sufficient.

164199.explanation: Long-term sustainability is our priority. We are open to both a rescue plan and bankruptcy. If bankruptcy costs us the least money and services continue, it is an option.

164200.explanation: Education comes first. We do not want teachers to have to clean up messes left by other users during their workday. If using school buildings in the evenings can happen without issues, we welcome that. We see this as the choice and responsibility of the schools themselves.

164201.explanation: Currently, school swimming is not financially supported by the municipality. There is no space in the budget. We are positive about researching the possibilities.

164202.explanation: The municipality is already considering various solutions. The current temporary solution, still using existing locations, meets our needs for now. A well-supported future plan could change our position.

164208.explanation: Housing our residents is very important. Attention must be given to all groups (starters, seniors, etc.). Social housing is one aspect. The priority now is mainly speeding up the implementation of existing plans.

164210.explanation: The principle is that homes should be built for owner-occupation. Buying for (social) rental should be possible if it benefits our residents.

164211.explanation: Using vacant buildings for housing can be a solution for the current housing shortage. Subsidizing this is not a long-term solution for us.

164212.explanation: For the VVD, both living and recreation are important. We want to be flexible with regulations and think along with the wishes of our residents. Of course, we want to create/build more homes, but we also want to retain sufficient recreational homes.

164214.explanation: More new housing facilities for the elderly promote flow-through, making more housing available for other seekers.

164215.explanation: Building homes is our priority. So this can definitely help us achieve our goals. We support flexible regulations in this area.

164216.explanation: This is not a starting point of our policy. For now, we have acquired enough land to meet our current goals. But we are always open to external opportunities.

164217.explanation: We support the current policy that provides support up to a maximum of 110% of the social assistance standard (income limit). Raising this limit costs too much money. Moreover, our priority would be better helping people within this 110% limit.

164218.explanation: Not everyone can find suitable work. However, everyone receiving benefits should be willing to make an effort. Of course, efforts must suit the possibilities of the individuals involved.

164220.explanation: The VVD believes everyone with social assistance should strive for a paid job. Of course, exceptions must be allowed if circumstances require. Providing informal care could be such an exception, but only if it concerns full-time care.

164222.explanation: As VVD, we favor less administrative burden. Filing the same application annually does not fit that image. However, we do believe that every year there should be a check on whether residents do or do not qualify (possibly retrospectively).

164223.explanation: We think it's important that everyone can contribute to the energy transition in their own way. However, there are already enough opportunities for support. We are always open to customized solutions in special circumstances as well as new initiatives.

164224.explanation: The VVD supports the use of electric cars. However, we believe there is currently sufficient supply.

164225.explanation: Public transport and good connections are important, especially for students. However, the route via Germany to Nijmegen and Arnhem is quite a detour. We also pay attention to connections with Germany.

164226.explanation: A low maximum speed contributes to traffic safety. We support the new mobility plan where a 30 km/h maximum speed in built-up areas is the standard. However, exceptions should be possible on through-roads. So not 30 km/h maximum on all roads.

164227.explanation: Speed bumps generally slow down traffic. In rural areas, we believe there are better ways to limit speed.

164228.explanation: Heavy traffic in our town centers does not contribute to traffic safety. However, destination traffic must remain possible. Therefore, we disagree.

164231.explanation: We are proud to be the most accessible municipality in the Netherlands. However, we see workplace measures as the responsibility of the entrepreneur. Of course, we are open to thinking along.

164233.explanation: We believe that the national government should take responsibility here. Children should never become victims of insufficient budgets.

164172.explanation: ADA would like to hear the residents through a referendum on various topics that concern all citizens.

164173.explanation: The management of the park should indeed be able to be taken over. At the moment, this is also policy and residents are provided with plants and advice to properly maintain the small piece of land.

164174.explanation: This has now been determined and should certainly not be increased.

164176.explanation: There is a citizens' council: the municipal council. All elected with an important task. We do want to hold a non-binding referendum on important issues that concern many people.

164177.explanation: The amounts should not increase, but the income should. ADA wants to give the rest of the world a look at our beautiful region and gladly reap the (tourist tax) benefits.

164178.explanation: Money that the national government provides to the municipality is given for a reason: if we start building reserves, we are shortchanging our residents. Saving is not a task of the municipality.

164179.explanation: There is currently too little space for entrepreneurs, and that is not beneficial for the local economy and employment.

164182.explanation: A wonderful initiative and possibly also very profitable. People who want to do this can access national schemes or finance it themselves. As far as ADA is concerned, this role does not lie with the municipality.

164183.explanation: Culture and development may cost money. That is beyond doubt for ADA. We do want a fair distribution of funds and good policy to implement that cultural development. And that is not always at the DRU.

164185.explanation: Nothing is free. Then these costs would shift to the waste disposal charges. We are in favor of pay-as-you-throw after sorting and want a thorough investigation into this.

164186.explanation: In the rural areas, there is certainly room for more nature. There can sometimes be less mowing there. Within residential neighborhoods, this can lead to dangerous situations, health complaints, and messy conditions.

164187.explanation: Agricultural land can be used much better and more sustainably. Solar panels are not circular and belong on roofs. Let's especially look at other solutions such as small wind turbines on private land.

164188.explanation: This should be determined by national policy. Encouraging it is a task that the municipality can take on.

164190.explanation: There is no thorough recent research; that is needed.

164191.explanation: We live in a beautiful municipality with lots of space and greenery. Let's use this space outside the urban centers as off-leash areas for dogs. This can partly be a field, but ADA also aims for an off-leash area by the water.

164192.explanation: This must be a strong lobby from our rural municipalities to The Hague. The approach must be implemented nationally (or at the European level).

164193.explanation: As far as ADA is concerned, there are enough wind turbines in our municipality. We more than meet the requirements set by national and regional policy.

164194.explanation: Our municipality currently spends what is requested. That is good. We want newcomers to be able to participate and be taught up to the level of the language requirement. If this level is not reached, it is good to maintain the language requirement.

164195.explanation: An asylum seeker village should only be for our own task. The assignment of refugees that Doetinchem must accommodate, they must be accommodated there.

164196.explanation: Considerable investment is already being made in the school buildings in our municipality. We must continue with that. It is good to agree on a clear IHP (integral housing plan) and create facilities accordingly.

164197.explanation: Achterhoeks is a beautiful dialect. To preserve it, school is a good place to pay attention to it. In recent years, wonderful projects and teaching materials have been developed for this.

164198.explanation: To properly represent our region, reporting on local activities and politics is very important. You cannot leave that to other broadcasters who have more areas under their responsibility.

164199.explanation: If it turns out there is no way forward, bankruptcy is an option. Other options should always be explored.

164200.explanation: Community buildings should be used as much as possible for multiple purposes.

164201.explanation: School swimming here can only be done in commercial pools since we no longer have a municipal pool. This would therefore cause unfair competition. Moreover, schools are extremely busy with this, while this task should lie with the educators.

164202.explanation: It is good to look at the possibilities that are currently available. Funds made available must be in line with other comparable sports clubs.

164208.explanation: We find the national norm of 28% fine for our municipality. (We are currently 4% below this) Our municipality needs middle incomes. We can attract these by investing in their homes.

164210.explanation: If we want to provide housing for middle incomes, investment parties must also invest in this.

164211.explanation: These buildings should definitely be able to be converted into homes given the housing shortage. According to ADA, subsidy is not needed for this. Converting and selling or renting the spaces will generate enough money.

164212.explanation: This must not become a standard practice. Many of these homes are not suitable for permanent stays. We also see nationwide that this causes a lot of inflow and turnover which is not monitored.

164214.explanation: Good apartments with the right facilities could ensure that our elderly choose to move there and leave their own house. This promotes flow and provides space for those looking for housing.

164215.explanation: National policy, implementation and action against the housing shortage. Also, informal care for the elderly on their own property becomes easier.

164216.explanation: Our municipality has land in different locations. Now that the pressure on the housing market is so high, it is good if we start doing something with it ourselves. Provide our own housing construction and design of village centers.

164217.explanation: Income policy is national policy. We patch up the wounds in urgent situations but do not want to undermine national politics or further open the poverty trap.

164218.explanation: It would be good if people who can and are on social assistance contribute to their environment. People who cannot do this should not be repeatedly expected to do something that is not feasible. The situation is often already sad enough.

164220.explanation: This always requires an individual assessment. No policy should be made on this, only methods of handling.

164222.explanation: An assessment is the basis for this. So it is necessary to do this annually.

164223.explanation: There are national subsidies for this. The municipality should not take this up, but there must be lobbying for the preservation or expansion of these schemes.

164224.explanation: It would be good if there were more charging points for electric cars in our municipality. There is already a municipal charging vision that ended in 2025 and plans for new poles until around 2027.

164225.explanation: Our residents must be able to use the train connection Kleve-Nijmegen. Therefore, a strong lobby is needed for the Oude IJsselstreek-Kleve connection.

164226.explanation: Where necessary, we support a 30 km/h zone. Implementing this everywhere, especially on through roads, seems pointless and frustrating to us.

164227.explanation: For the farmers in our municipality, these speed bumps are very obstructive and dangerous.

164228.explanation: ADA wants to limit heavy traffic through village centers as much as possible. So only destination traffic. Banning transit traffic through village centers makes it unworkable for our farmers and retailers.

164231.explanation: Municipal buildings must all be made fully accessible. For buildings belonging to entrepreneurs and private individuals, this is certainly desirable. It is not up to the municipality to finance this, but it should encourage it.

164233.explanation: We applaud the municipality's efficient management of the youth care budget. We want to maintain this, taking into account that professionals continue to determine the care needed for our youth.

164172.explanation: Although a referendum can be useful in some cases, many issues are too complex to reduce to a multiple-choice question. D66 advocates the creation of a citizens' council for such topics: a group of randomly selected residents who, supported by experts, provide advice to the municipal council.

164173.explanation: Residents often do not feel involved in the maintenance of green strips. D66 wants residents and businesses to be able to adopt green strips and receive a contribution towards maintenance costs.

164174.explanation: The property tax rates in Oude IJsselstreek are comparable to the Dutch average for residences, and significantly lower than average for non-residential properties. D66 wants to reduce the municipality's debt burden before considering lowering property taxes.

164176.explanation: D66 wants a citizens' council to be formed early on in major developments. This group of randomly selected citizens receives information from experts and advises the municipal council.

164177.explanation: D66 supports forms of tourism that match the qualities of our municipality. Tourists mainly come to experience peace, nature, and water. If the municipality spends more money to promote tourism, this could justify raising the tourist tax, but that is not a goal in itself.

164178.explanation: The municipality has a large loan debt. D66 wants windfalls to be primarily used to repay debts so that the loan portfolio is reduced.

164179.explanation: The municipality already has plans to develop more business parks. For D66, this is sufficient for now.

164182.explanation: Investing in generating sustainable energy yields returns even without financial support from the municipality. However, D66 supports providing loans for sustainability measures such as generating sustainable energy.

164183.explanation: D66 wants to continue the DRU Industry Park foundation in a streamlined form: a foundation that organizes cultural activities with a focus on local talent. The municipality can subsidize these activities linked to concrete goals.

164185.explanation: D66 wants to move towards a circular economy with the reuse of materials and resources. Making bulky waste disposal free removes a financial incentive to handle materials more sustainably. Moreover, the processing costs of waste should then be paid by all residents.

164186.explanation: D66 wants parts of the roadside verges to remain standing longer and for cuttings to be removed. This increases biodiversity by giving flowers and herbs a greater chance, providing food and shelter for insects, birds, and small animals. Of course, only where traffic safety allows.

164187.explanation: The demand for electricity continues to rise, partly due to heat pumps and electric cars. D66 therefore wants to permit solar fields. Care must be taken that they fit into the landscape, and where possible, double use of the land is preferred.

164188.explanation: The municipality can inform farmers about the subsidy scheme 'Investing in Integrated Crop Protection' from the Netherlands Enterprise Agency. The municipality should not provide subsidies itself.

164190.explanation: Post-sorting is easier for residents, but its disadvantage is that machines cannot yet properly separate all recyclable materials. Therefore, source separation still provides the highest environmental benefit. D66 currently does not consider this research useful.

164191.explanation: D66 wants to allow off-leash areas for dogs in communities where there is demand. Preferably in cooperation with dog owners, sharing responsibility for maintenance and cleanliness.

164192.explanation: D66 wants a pesticide-free zone of 50 meters around homes, not only for new construction projects but also for existing buildings.

164193.explanation: The demand for electricity continues to rise, partly due to heat pumps and electric cars. To continue generating sufficient sustainable energy in the future, both solar and wind energy remain necessary. D66 is therefore open to new initiatives as they arise.

164194.explanation: Immigration and integration are conditions for newcomers to succeed in their new environment. Moreover, the municipality receives funding from the national government for this. D66 therefore considers it unwise to cut back on this.

164195.explanation: D66 regards asylum seeker reception as a moral duty. By realizing an asylum seeker center of this scale, instead of dispersing over the communities, the COA pays for facilities such as a school and general practitioner.

164196.explanation: Students learn and grow best in modern, fresh buildings. The new IKC in Gendringen is a good example of such a modern building. In other communities, money will also need to be invested in new school buildings in the coming years.

164197.explanation: Many primary schools already pay attention to the Achterhoek dialect by offering Wiesneus lessons. D66 sees promoting the use of this as primarily a task for the Achterhoek Region rather than the municipality.

164198.explanation: Oude IJsselstreek provides the highest contribution per resident to the regional broadcaster Regio8 of all participating municipalities, namely €2.50. That is the amount Regio8 requests. For example, the municipality of Doetinchem provides only half that amount.

164199.explanation: In the event of bankruptcy, the municipality loses control of the DRU, creating risks for suppliers and employees. D66 wants to continue the DRU Industry Park foundation in a streamlined form: a foundation that organizes cultural activities with a focus on local talent.

164200.explanation: School buildings are funded with public money. Parts of the school building that lend themselves to this (such as the gymnasium) can be used by associations outside school hours, of course with good agreements.

164201.explanation: Oude IJsselstreek is a water-rich municipality. D66 finds it important that children learn to swim well, maintain their swimming skills, and can save themselves.

164202.explanation: The municipality should not allocate money for a completely new sports park for SV Wisch, but should make one of the current two locations suitable for the merged club. This concerns expanding the changing rooms and laying a new grass field.

164208.explanation: Project developers prefer to build owner-occupied homes, while there is a shortage of rental homes. D66 wants at least 30% of homes in new construction projects to be social rental housing.

164210.explanation: For new construction projects, D66 sees a self-occupancy obligation contractually fixed for a number of years (via a chain clause) as a good means to combat speculation, price inflation, and exploitative practices.

164211.explanation: Vacant buildings remain unused while there is a housing shortage. D66 wants the municipality to encourage owners to convert these buildings into housing. The municipality helps remove obstacles and can also provide subsidies, for example, to create more social rental housing.

164212.explanation: Allowing holiday homes to be used as permanent addresses removes them from the recreational supply. Moreover, holiday parks are often not equipped for permanent residence.

164214.explanation: By building not only starter homes but also senior homes, families can move into homes that have become too large for seniors, thus freeing up starter homes for new starters again.

164215.explanation: D66 wants residents to be able to place a temporary, movable home in their garden without a permit to provide informal care.

164216.explanation: D66 finds it unwise for the municipality to take large financial risks by speculating on land on a large scale.

164217.explanation: Raising the income threshold for minimum schemes results in substantial costs and is inefficient because people who do not really need it can also make use of these schemes. D66 wants a gradual phase-out from the current income threshold along with tailored support.

164218.explanation: With social assistance, the focus should not be on providing a counter-performance, but on developing and guiding people. Socially meaningful volunteer work can often contribute well to this, but it remains customized. D66 does not want to make this mandatory.

164220.explanation: People receiving social assistance benefits who perform intensive informal care tasks that cannot be combined with paid employment or reintegration projects should be able to obtain a temporary exemption from the job application requirement, similar to those receiving unemployment benefits. This should be assessed individually on a case-by-case basis.

164222.explanation: For many people with benefits, their situation changes little from year to year. In such cases, a few simple questions to determine whether there has been a change in circumstances should be sufficient to remain eligible for minimum social provisions.

164223.explanation: Energy expenses have increased enormously in recent years. These extra costs primarily affect people with low incomes, who often live in homes that are less well insulated and who cannot fully afford insulation work themselves.

164224.explanation: D66 wants charging stations for electric cars to be installed in every town and village.

164225.explanation: D66 wants the municipality to commit to improving dense public transport within the municipality, and to good connections with neighboring municipalities. Good connections with Nijmegen and Arnhem are not an end in themselves but a logical consequence. Those connections can also run through Germany.

164226.explanation: For traffic safety, D66 wants 30 km/h to become the standard speed limit within built-up areas. This also means that the design of these roads will be adapted to prevent speeding.

164227.explanation: To ensure safety on roads outside built-up areas, for example for cycling students, speed-reducing measures are sometimes needed. In some cases, these will be speed bumps. Preferably, D66 would like to see more cycle paths that are physically separated from the roadway.

164228.explanation: D66 wants heavy truck traffic and large agricultural vehicles to be routed around residential centers as much as possible. Alternative routes for heavy traffic will be arranged together with the province and neighboring municipalities.

164231.explanation: D66 considers accessibility of buildings important, but does not believe it is the municipality’s task to financially support entrepreneurs in this. The municipality can, however, provide advice and cooperate with entrepreneurs.

164233.explanation: The municipality must do what is necessary, whether that costs more or less than the contribution from the national government. D66 finds it important to focus on early detection and prevention, to reduce the pressure on youth care and to lower costs.

164172.explanation: Yes, the interests of the citizens come first. A referendum is particularly suitable for this purpose.

164173.explanation: No, considering past experiences, the maintenance of public parks should remain a municipal responsibility.

164174.explanation: Yes, increases should keep pace with inflation.

164178.explanation: Yes, without general reserves we are vulnerable to setbacks.

164179.explanation: Yes, employment brings life to our municipality.

164183.explanation: In its current form, the DRU is not viable. After investigation, the DRU could potentially make a fresh start in a slimmed-down form and with a limited budget.

164185.explanation: No, as long as cooperation via Kringloop Aktief exists, there are sufficient opportunities to have reusable items collected for free. Residents must pay for bulky waste disposal.

164186.explanation: No, traffic safety must not be compromised. Exotic species should be actively addressed.

164187.explanation: As long as alternatives exist, these should be used for solar panel placement, such as on roofs of commercial buildings.

164188.explanation: No, we adhere to national regulations.

164191.explanation: Yes, depending on a pilot field, decisions will be made on whether this is the solution.

164192.explanation: Yes, if public health is at risk, and no when no alternative is available.

164194.explanation: We would like to make use of the current possibilities where volunteers play an important role.

164195.explanation: We oppose one large asylum seekers’ village and choose for small-scale reception locations.

164196.explanation: Yes, we have been on the right track in recent years.

164197.explanation: No, no additional tasks for education.

164199.explanation: If this is an unavoidable result of the policy pursued and there is no viable alternative without additional financial injection.

164201.explanation: We are considering the possibility for the municipality to offer discounts for swimming lessons.

164202.explanation: The submitted plan of SV Wisch for the expansion of De Munsterman should be approved.

164208.explanation: Yes, building according to the needs of our citizens to prevent long waiting lists.

164210.explanation: Even for people who do not (yet) live in the municipality of Oude IJsselstroom, it should be possible to purchase a home in our municipality.

164211.explanation: Yes, provided that the solution does not impede other users.

164214.explanation: No, more bungalows for our elderly.

164215.explanation: Yes. Care homes and homes for children should be made possible.

164216.explanation: Yes. Depending on the number of years, the municipality can anticipate accordingly.

164217.explanation: No, retain the existing arrangement with annual evaluation.

164218.explanation: This already happens, depending on personal circumstances.

164220.explanation: Yes, but depending on the intensity of the care.

164224.explanation: Yes, if there is a need for more public charging stations.

164225.explanation: Yes, work is already underway within the Euregio partnership.

164226.explanation: Traffic flow on our main roads should remain unchanged.

164231.explanation: No. We do not see this as a task for the municipality.

164233.explanation: In principle, the national budget should be sufficient. We apply customized solutions at bottlenecks.

statements.title: Oude IJsselstreek should cooperate with plans for new wind turbines.

titleParts.text: Oude IJsselstreek moet meewerken aan plannen voor nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: These are modern wind turbines that convert wind power into electricity and can be up to 150 meters high.

statements.moreInfo: The municipality of Oude IJsselstreek has made agreements with the province and the region about generating sustainable energy in the so‑called Regional Energy Strategy (RES). Some agreements concern wind turbines. Participating in plans for those means the municipality investigates locations, grants permits and talks with residents. Other governments also have legal powers, but the municipal council decides whether and where new wind turbines will be placed.
statements.pro: Some parties believe more wind turbines are needed to generate more renewable energy in Oude IJsselstreek. Furthermore, parties argue that more wind turbines are necessary to meet the targets of the Regional Energy Strategy (RES).
statements.con: Other parties argue that the municipality has already invested a lot in wind turbines compared with other municipalities in the region. There is also a party that believes there are enough wind turbines in Oude IJsselstreek and that the municipality complies with regional and national policy.

statements.title: The municipality must mow roadside verges and grass fields less often.
statements.moreInfo: Along municipal roads there are verges and in neighborhoods and parks there are grass areas. The municipality is responsible for their maintenance and decides per location how often they are mown.
statements.pro: Some parties believe mowing less frequently is better for nature because it creates greater diversity of plants and animals. It is also said that mowing less often leads to a healthier living environment. Furthermore, one party believes mowing less is more sustainable: mowing machines are used less, causing less wear and consuming less energy.
statements.con: Other parties argue that mowing less can obstruct drivers’ visibility. There is also a party that believes common ragwort (sint-jacobskruiskruid), which is poisonous to animals, will spread more easily with less mowing. It is also argued that less-mown verges and grass areas often look untidy.

statements.title: The municipality should establish a citizens' assembly to advise on important issues.

titleParts.glossary: A group of residents chosen by lot that advises politicians or helps decide on a specific issue.

titleParts.text: instellen, dat adviseert over belangrijke onderwerpen.

statements.moreInfo: A citizens' assembly is a randomly selected group of residents who advise politicians or help decide on a specific issue. They receive information from experts and have discussions with each other. The municipal council can use this advice when making decisions, but is not bound by it.
statements.pro: Some parties think a citizens' assembly provides better advice on important decisions. They also believe a citizens' assembly increases residents' involvement.
statements.con: Another party believes the municipal council already represents residents and that a citizens’ assembly is therefore unnecessary. There is also a party that thinks a citizens’ assembly is very costly.

statements.title: In our municipality there should be only one asylum-seeker village in the rural area, with 448 places.

titleParts.text: In onze gemeente moet slechts op één locatie in het buitengebied een

titleParts.glossary: Reception center for asylum seekers. They live there until it is decided whether they may stay in the Netherlands. An asylum seekers' village remains in a municipality for a longer period.
Space for people who need help.

statements.moreInfo: Municipalities are legally obliged to accommodate asylum seekers. To meet that obligation Oude IJsselstreek plans to set up a reception location in the countryside with 448 places. Residents are involved in the plans for this so‑called asylum seekers' village through information evenings and public consultation.
statements.pro: Some parties point out that that agreement has already been made in the so-called Koersdocument. One party argues that the Central Agency for the Reception of Asylum Seekers (COA) will provide facilities such as a school and a GP in a large asylum seekers' village.
statements.con: Other parties believe Oude IJsselstreek should not adopt the objectives of other municipalities. There is also a party that thinks Oude IJsselstreek should organise asylum reception in a humane and careful way, with distribution and public support.

statements.title: Residents should be able to take over the maintenance of a public green space from the municipality and receive funding for it.
statements.moreInfo: Residents of Oude IJsselstreek can maintain a municipal green space themselves. In that case the municipality makes agreements with them about the maintenance, for example regarding safety and compensation. The municipality remains responsible.
statements.pro: Some parties believe this measure increases involvement and strengthens social cohesion.
statements.con: Another party points out that in the past bad experiences were had with this measure.

statements.title: The tourist tax should be increased.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.moreInfo: Tourist tax is a municipal tax for tourists who stay overnight in hotels, campsites or holiday homes. The municipality can use the income from tourist tax for any municipal tasks.
statements.pro: Some parties believe the municipality can develop and implement new plans with additional income.
statements.con: Other parties think a higher tourist tax deters tourists, while Oude IJsselstreek actually wants to attract more tourists.

statements.title: The municipality should support more people in poverty by raising the income threshold for assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet meer mensen in armoede ondersteunen door de

titleParts.glossary: The maximum amount you are allowed to earn, for example to qualify for assistance.

statements.moreInfo: Oude IJsselstreek supports residents with an income below a certain threshold with schemes for care, school costs or energy. By raising that income threshold the municipality can support more residents.
statements.pro: One party believes the municipality can better address the hidden poverty of residents just above the current income threshold in this way. Another party points out that an increasing number of residents have difficulty making ends meet after paying fixed costs.
statements.con: There is also a party that believes income policy is not the municipality’s task but the national government’s. Finally, it is said that raising the income threshold will cost the municipality a lot of money.

statements.title: In the five large villages the municipality should create a field where dogs may run off-leash.
statements.moreInfo: Off‑leash fields are places where dogs may run free. Suitable locations are needed for their creation. Building and maintaining them costs money. The municipality often involves residents in choosing sites for off‑leash fields.
statements.pro: Some parties believe that off-leash dog areas lead to less nuisance elsewhere. One party thinks off-leash areas are important for dogs to get enough exercise.
statements.con: Other parties believe creating off‑leash dog areas is not a municipal responsibility. One party also thinks dogs are better off being allowed off‑leash outside the villages.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.moreInfo: Homeowners can install solar panels on their roof if it is suitable. If that is not possible, people can join others to buy or rent land for one or more wind turbines or solar parks so they can still invest in sustainable energy. Sustainable energy is generated from sources that never run out, such as sunlight and wind. Collective generation of sustainable energy often takes place in the form of energy cooperatives.
statements.pro: Some parties believe financial support lowers the barrier to making things more sustainable. They also think residents will become less dependent on energy companies.
statements.con: Other parties believe supporting sustainable energy generation is not a municipal task. One party also considers the national subsidies for sustainable energy sufficient.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

titleParts.text: krijgen, moeten in ruil daarvoor maatschappelijk zinvol werk doen.

statements.moreInfo: People who have no job or no other benefit can apply to the municipality for a social assistance benefit (bijstandsuitkering). In some municipalities they are required to perform a community service in return. That must suit their abilities. They then do socially useful work for which they are not paid. The municipality determines how this arrangement is implemented.
statements.pro: Some parties believe residents receiving benefits have a duty to contribute to society.
statements.con: Other parties believe a mandatory reciprocal obligation does not help residents progress.

statements.title: The municipality should encourage all primary schools to include the Achterhoek dialect.
statements.moreInfo: the Achterhoeks dialect is cultural heritage. By paying attention to it in primary schools, children in Oude IJsselstreek can learn about Achterhoeks and its history. This supplements the regular curriculum.
statements.pro: Some parties believe attention to the Achterhoeks dialect strengthens cultural identity and regional bonds. One party also considers Achterhoeks a beautiful dialect.
statements.con: Other parties believe schools should not be given additional tasks. One party also thinks the municipality should not interfere with school curricula.

statements.title: The municipality should ban solar panels on agricultural land.
