titleParts.glossary: Land that farmers use for keeping livestock or growing food.

statements.moreInfo: The national government and the province have set strict rules for placing solar panels on agricultural land. As a result it is usually not allowed to create large fields of solar panels. Small arrays of solar panels for personal use are often permitted. The municipality Oude IJsselstreek can choose to completely forbid new solar panels on agricultural land or to allow them.
statements.pro: Some parties think all roofs should first be covered with solar panels. One party believes agricultural land can be used better and more sustainably. It is also said that solar panels are not circular.
statements.con: Other parties believe new solar panels meet the growing need for sustainable energy. One party thinks there are already enough solar panels in the municipality. Finally, it is mentioned that small‑scale applications should be possible.

statements.title: The municipality should give low-income households more money to insulate their homes.
statements.moreInfo: In the Netherlands we have agreed to emit fewer greenhouse gases such as CO₂ into the atmosphere, to limit global warming and meet climate targets. In well‑insulated homes less energy is needed to heat them. That lowers energy bills and CO₂ emissions.
statements.pro: Some parties believe this extra support helps low-income residents insulate their homes, which lowers their energy bills.
statements.con: Other parties believe there are sufficient national schemes for insulating homes.

statements.title: Vacant offices should be converted into housing with subsidies from the municipality.
statements.moreInfo: Converting vacant offices into homes can make housing available quickly without new construction. The municipality can stimulate this process by providing subsidies and adjusting regulations.
statements.pro: Some parties believe converting vacant offices reduces the housing shortage. They also think this measure prevents vacancy in Oude IJsselstreek.
statements.con: Other parties believe that adjusting regulations is sufficient to convert more vacant office buildings; providing money is not necessary. One party also believes office building owners already earn enough from converting and selling them.

statements.title: The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.
statements.moreInfo: School swimming is teaching children to swim through school so they can be safe in the water. In the past children in many municipalities could learn to swim via school. Nowadays parents often arrange this themselves, but as a result not all children obtain a swimming certificate.
statements.pro: Some parties believe all children should be able to save themselves in the water. They also say swimming lessons are expensive for families and that all children deserve equal opportunities.
statements.con: Other parties believe it is the responsibility of parents to ensure children can swim, not the schools. One party also thinks school swimming does not fit into primary school curricula. Finally, it is argued that the municipality has no money for school swimming.

statements.title: The maximum speed on all roads within built-up areas should be 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.pro: Some parties believe a 30 km/h speed limit is safer for cyclists and pedestrians. They also think a lower speed limit improves livability.
statements.con: Other parties believe traffic on through roads should be able to keep moving smoothly. One party also thinks a lower speed limit is not necessary everywhere within built‑up areas.

statements.title: The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
statements.pro: One party believes the municipality should be flexible with rules. It is also argued that the municipality should think along with residents’ wishes.
statements.con: Some parties believe many holiday homes and their surroundings are not suitable for permanent residence. Another party thinks the number of holiday cottages should not decrease.

statements.title: The municipality should allocate funds for a new sports park for SV Wisch.
statements.moreInfo: The football club SV Wisch currently plays at sports parks De Munsterman (Silvolde) and De Paasberg (Terborg). There is debate about the club’s future accommodation. The municipality must weigh interests between sport and health on the one hand and the costs of building and maintaining a possible new sports park on the other.
statements.pro: Some parties believe good sports facilities contribute to residents’ health. One party also believes sport brings the residents of Oude IJsselstreek together.
statements.con: Other parties believe the current situation is satisfactory. One party also thinks expanding sports park De Munsterman is a better alternative than building a new sports park.

statements.title: Tractors and trucks should not be allowed to drive through village centers.
statements.pro: Some parties believe banning tractors and trucks from village centers increases traffic safety. They also think it reduces nuisance in village centers and that the municipality should develop alternative routes for freight together with the province and neighboring municipalities.
statements.con: Other parties believe the measure is unworkable for farmers and retail. One party thinks this cannot always be avoided. There are also parties that believe destination traffic may pass through village centers because there is no alternative.

statements.title: The municipality should advocate for a ban on the use of agricultural pesticides near residential areas.
statements.moreInfo: Pesticides can be harmful to people and nature. Municipalities can therefore advocate limiting their use near homes. National rules ultimately determine what is and is not permitted.
statements.pro: Some parties believe such a ban is necessary for public health and livability. One party thinks a 50-meter spray-free zone from buildings is needed. It is also said Oude IJsselstreek should lobby in The Hague with other rural municipalities to enforce this.
statements.con: Other parties believe there is no clear reason for it. One party thinks national rules for pesticide use are sufficient. Finally, one party notes that “there is no farmer who sprays poison on his land.”

statements.title: Anyone who buys a home in our municipality must live in it themselves.
statements.moreInfo: The municipality can introduce an owner‑occupancy requirement. That is the obligation that homeowners must live in the house they bought themselves. They may not rent it out.
statements.pro: One party believes houses are for living in, not for making a profit. Other parties think Oude IJsselstreek should counteract speculation on homes.
statements.con: Other parties believe the owner‑occupancy requirement reduces the supply of rental homes for middle‑income residents. One party also believes people who are not going to live in Oude IJsselstreek should still be free to buy a house there.

statements.title: The municipality should spend more on youth care than it receives from the national government.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet meer geld uitgeven aan

titleParts.glossary: Support for young people with problems, for example mental health care, foster care, day programmes, and help with dyslexia.

titleParts.text: dan wat ze daarvoor krijgt van het Rijk.

statements.moreInfo: The national government allocates funding for youth care to the municipality, but this amount is often insufficient to cover the costs of youth care. The municipality can choose to spend additional funds itself, for example to reduce waiting lists in youth care.
statements.pro: Some parties believe children and young people deserve the care they need. They also think money should not stand in the way of the right treatment.
statements.con: Other parties believe the municipality should use available funds efficiently. One party also believes the national government must take responsibility and provide sufficient funding for youth care.

statements.title: The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet ondernemers financieel helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.moreInfo: Residents with a disability can participate more easily if buildings in Oude IJsselstreek are accessible to them. The municipality can choose to give entrepreneurs subsidies to make their buildings more accessible, for example by installing ramps.
statements.pro: Some parties believe it is important that as many buildings as possible in the municipality are accessible to everyone.
statements.con: Other parties believe making buildings more accessible is not the municipality’s task, but the responsibility of the businesses themselves.

statements.title: The municipality should not install new speed bumps on roads outside built-up areas.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geen nieuwe verkeersdrempels in wegen buiten de

titleParts.glossary: An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.moreInfo: Speed bumps cause vehicles to drive more slowly. The municipality Oude IJsselstreek weighs traffic safety against traffic flow for each road.
statements.pro: Some parties believe speed bumps are dangerous for agricultural vehicles. They also think there are better means to limit speed.
statements.con: Other parties believe speed bumps increase road safety. One party also thinks separated bicycle paths are often more effective.

statements.title: The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.moreInfo: Property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) is the tax residents pay as owners of a building or piece of land, or as users of a commercial property. If Oude IJsselstreek lowers the property tax, the municipality receives less revenue. That can affect the quality of municipal services.
statements.pro: One party believes the property tax (OZB) can be returned to its previous level once there is room in the budget.
statements.con: Other parties consider preserving municipal services important, and therefore a tax cut is not realistic.

statements.title: The municipality should spend less than its income in order to build up a reserve.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet minder geld uitgeven dan er binnenkomt, om een

statements.moreInfo: The municipality can choose to spend less money than it receives. That way it can save for a reserve, but less remains available for carrying out plans.
statements.pro: Some parties believe it is important that the municipality has sufficient reserves to absorb setbacks.
statements.con: Other parties believe the municipality should use tax revenues directly for projects that benefit its residents, not for saving.

statements.title: The municipality should reduce spending on the integration of newcomers into society.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet minder geld uitgeven aan

titleParts.glossary: Newcomers learn the language and customs of the Netherlands.
People who are new to the Netherlands.

statements.moreInfo: The municipality helps newcomers with language and work as part of its integration policy. Within the limits of the law it can choose to spend less money, which leads to less support for newcomers.
statements.pro: One party believes the current options with volunteers are sufficient.
statements.con: Other parties believe that newcomers integrating well is important to be able to participate in society and for social cohesion.

statements.title: The municipality should itself purchase land around village centers where homes or businesses can be built in the future.
statements.moreInfo: When a municipality buys land itself to steer housing development and business activity, this is called “active land policy” (actief grondbeleid).
statements.pro: Some parties believe the municipality can better determine where, what and for whom is built through active land policy. One party adds that this allows building faster and cheaper.
statements.con: Other parties believe active land policy carries large financial risks. There is also a party that finds this unnecessary because the municipality already owns enough land.

statements.title: The municipality may allow the DRU Industriepark foundation to go bankrupt.
statements.moreInfo: The DRU Industriepark foundation manages the cultural facilities on the site of the former DRU factory in Ulft. If this foundation goes bankrupt, cultural activities will disappear or others will have to take them over. The municipality balances cultural value against costs.
statements.pro: Some parties believe bankruptcy offers a perspective for a healthy future for the DRU. They also say this does not mean the entire DRU will disappear and that bankruptcy is the inevitable result of the policy pursued.
statements.con: Other parties believe the municipality would lose control of the DRU in case of bankruptcy. One party also thinks this carries risks for suppliers and employees. Finally, one party argues that the DRU is an indispensable cultural and social facility.

statements.title: In Oude IJsselstreek more business parks should be developed than currently planned.
statements.moreInfo: The municipality Oude IJsselstreek can opt to develop additional business parks and, together with the region, decide where they will be located. In doing so the municipality must weigh, among other things, employment benefits against the space required and investments needed.
statements.pro: Some parties believe more business parks are needed for the economy and employment in Oude IJsselstreek.
statements.con: Other parties believe there are already sufficient plans for new business parks in the municipality.

shootoutStatements.title: The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.
The municipality should allow the construction of a small second dwelling in gardens.
Oude IJsselstreek should investigate whether separating household waste after collection is cheaper.
The municipality should install more charging stations for electric cars.
At least 33% of newly built homes in Oude IJsselstreek should be social rental housing. Currently 28% of homes in the Netherlands are social rental housing.
Residents of Oude IJsselstreek should be able to express their opinion on important issues through a referendum.
If farmers do not spray pesticides on land near buildings, the municipality should pay them for that.
Primary schools should be available for use by others in the evenings and on weekends.
The municipality should stop providing additional funding to the DRU Industriepark foundation.
More apartments for elderly people should be built in Oude IJsselstreek.
Bulky waste disposal should be free of charge.
Low-income residents who apply for additional benefits should have to do so every year.
The municipality should spend more money on improving school buildings during the next council term.
People receiving welfare benefits who provide informal care to a family member or acquaintance do not have to apply for jobs.

shootoutStatements.theme: Public transport link between Nijmegen and Arnhem
shootoutStatements.title: The municipality should work to establish a good public transport connection to Nijmegen and Arnhem via Germany.
